asan.evidence_summary: Real-library reproduction did not reach runtime because the shipped libupb.a archive lacks tokenizer objects and the ASan build also reported a version mismatch during link.

lldb.evidence_summary: Harness execution showed size_t wrap from SIZE_MAX-3 plus 8 to 4, reserve bypass, memcpy destination 4 bytes before storage, and UBSan pointer overflow at string.h:92.

fresh.evidence_summary: Independent review identified unchecked size_t arithmetic in the capacity check and growth formula leading to under-allocation before memcpy.

impact.evidence_summary: Practical effect was demonstrated as a stack-buffer-underflow and abort, but the vulnerable upb tokenizer path is only source-level in this snapshot and is not reachable from the shipped archive.

notes: Consensus supports the bug as a real integer-overflow-driven buffer write in upb_String_Append(), with reduced confidence only because the provided build artifacts do not expose a direct real-library reproduction path.
